item.title: The Peter McCormack Show вАУ #175 - Dr Melvin Vopson - A Physicist Says Reality May Be Code
item.description:
Physicist Professor Melvin Vopson joins Peter McCormack to discuss information physics, simulation theory, AI, digital life, consciousness, God, free will, and death.


Melvin argues that the universe may be governed by information and computation, and that recent advances in brain emulation could force us to confront one of the strangest questions imaginable: if a brain is loaded into a simulation, what exactly wakes up?


- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -


TIMESTAMPS:

00:00:00 вАФ If You Hack the Simulation, You Control the Universe!

00:01:25 вАФ Why Does Life Feel Artificial?

00:03:26 вАФ Are We Living in a Simulation?

00:05:45 вАФ What Is Information Physics?

00:10:09 вАФ Information as the Fifth State of Matter

00:11:18 вАФ John Wheeler and вАЬIt from BitвАЭ

00:13:33 вАФ Is DNA Just Information?

00:16:08 вАФ Who Created the Creator?

00:19:47 вАФ How Would Mario Prove HeвАЩs Inside the Game?

00:25:46 вАФ Does the Universe Optimise Itself?

00:29:35 вАФ Is Symmetry a Form of Compression?

00:30:40 вАФ GTA, Rendering, and Computational Reality

00:32:37 вАФ Error-Correcting Code in Physics

00:37:30 вАФ Could Information Explain Dark Matter?

00:40:24 вАФ If You Hack Reality, You Control the Universe

00:45:36 вАФ вАЬIn the Beginning Was the WordвАЭ

00:46:29 вАФ вАЬIn the Beginning Was the CodeвАЭ

00:57:20 вАФ What Exactly Wakes Up in There?

01:00:32 вАФ But Is It Me?

01:20:56 вАФ Would Proof of Simulation Change Anything?


- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -


CONTACT PETE

вАЇ Website вАУ http://petermccormack.com

вАЇ Email вАУ me@petermccormack.com

вАЇ Instagram вАУ /mccormack555

CONNECT WITH MELVIN VOPSON

вАЇ Website:

вАУ https://sites.google.com/site/melvinvopson/

вАУ https://www.port.ac.uk/about-us/structure-and-governance/our-people/our-staff/melvin-vopson


SPONSORS

item.title: The Why Bitcoin Show вАУ #100 - 100 Not Out: Reflections, Regrets and Why I'm Just Getting Started
item.description:
Episode 100. No intro. No ads. Just me.

This one is different. Just an honest reflection on 100 episodes, what this journey has actually been like, and where things are heading.

I share what drove me to start the show, the momentum I built and lost, the guests who genuinely changed how I think, and something personal I haven't said publicly before about confidence, doubt, and what it's taken to finally back myself.

I also explain why I built My Bitcoin Will, a DIY Bitcoin inheritance starter kit designed to help people get off zero and start closing the gap between law, access, and education.

If you're not ready to dive in yet, start with the free Bitcoin Inheritance Stress Test or download the checklist at My Bitcoin Will.

Also, some gentle shilling from my side. I've written a book. No Tenants, No Troubles: The Case for Digital Over Real Estate. Available on Amazon now: https://www.dalewarburton.com/book

Need a hand with self-custody? Book a session with me to get your Bitcoin security house in order.

And get on Nostr if you haven't already. It's a game changer. npub1r94hh3kt6re3j5jv7yg8rw0ah5fg90vw9pjklxzknvhdkx0famfsn0njzt

]]>

item.description:
Sascha Grumbach ist Gr√Љnder und CEO von Green Mining, einem Schweizer Unternehmen, das Bitcoin-Mining neu denkt. Bevor er selbst eine Mining-Firma aufgebaut hat, ist er auf Mining-Scams hereingefallen вАУ eine Erfahrung, die ihn gepr√§gt hat und die Grundlage f√Љr sein heutiges Gesch√§ftsmodell ist. item.title: Store of Bitcoin - Bitcoin as Store of Value вАУ Ep. 49 - Daniel Batten | A new different wave of Bitcoin Adopters.
item.description:
In this episode of Store of Bitcoin, host Alessandro Ottaviani welcomes Daniel Batten, a prominent Bitcoin analyst and investor, to discuss the evolving narrative surrounding Bitcoin, particularly in relation to energy consumption and environmental impact. Daniel shares insights on how the mainstream media's perception of Bitcoin has shifted from skepticism to a more favorable view, largely due to rigorous research and advocacy from the Bitcoin community. He emphasizes the importance of effectively communicating Bitcoin's benefits to various stakeholders, including policymakers and investors, to foster broader adoption.

The conversation delves into the challenges of reaching new adopters, particularly those who may not be motivated by the same values that initially attracted early Bitcoin enthusiasts. Daniel highlights the need for a more nuanced approach to education and outreach, focusing on the social and environmental benefits of Bitcoin. He also discusses the potential for Bitcoin to serve as a tool for financial inclusion in developing countries, where it can provide a safeguard against inflation and empower individuals in oppressive regimes. The episode concludes with a look at future trends in Bitcoin adoption and the importance of aligning messaging with the values of diverse audiences.

This episode is sponsored by вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†BitVaultвБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†вБ†, the new open-source & self-custody wallet utilizing time-delays and multisignature to offset physical attacks and hacks.

item.title: Bitcoin Archive w Archie вАУ $39 Trillion US Debt Bomb is Ticking | Lyn Alden
item.description:
Is The US really heading towards a $39 Trillion Collapse?

In this conversation with Lyn Alden, we explore one of the biggest macro shifts happening right now.

From BRICS nations, to alternative payment rails. Global trade is slowly diversifying away from a single dominant currency, and that shift could have massive implications for Bitcoin.

вЪ†пЄП This isnвАЩt a sudden collapse; itвАЩs a slow rebalancing of global power.

item.title: Bitcoin News Alerts | Daily BTC Macro Signal вАУ 2337: $12 Trillion Schwab Opens Bitcoin Trading - $10M LOCKOUT Begins
item.description:
Charles Schwab just opened Bitcoin trading to 40 million customers while institutional capital continues absorbing scarce supply. Strategy alone raised enough capital to acquire 5,525 BTC this week - roughly 12x more than miners created over the same period. This is why $10 million Bitcoin may happen far faster than most people think.

For the full premium livestream experience with video, visit our Rumble at http://BitcoinNewsAlerts.net

]]>

item.title: Rob Wallace | Bitcoin News вАУ The Oil Crisis Is Hiding a $4.5T Debt Bomb No One Can Pay | Infra
item.description:
The Biggest Bubble Ever? | Infra Explains Why Markets Are Breaking Upwards, Debt Is Exploding & Bitcoin Could Hit $10M

In this macro deep dive, Rob Wallace sits down with market analyst Robert (@infraa_) to unpack what may be the most dangerous financial environment in modern history.

Despite war in the Middle East, soaring oil prices, record sovereign debt, and mounting stress in global credit markets, stocks continue ripping higher. Why?

Infra argues that governments and central banks are desperately trying to keep the bubble alive through debt issuance, market intervention, liquidity injections, and financial engineering. But according to him, the system is reaching a breaking point.

]]>

item.title: THE Bitcoin Podcast вАУ NUMBER GO DOWN: Deflation, Bitcoin, and Communist Lies | Allen Farrington
item.description:
Allen Farrington returns to THE Bitcoin Podcast to discuss his latest essay вАЬNumber Go Down,вАЭ the myths surrounding deflation, why fiat economics gets causation backwards, and how inflationary monetary systems distort society itself.

Walker and Allen explore the вАЬparadox of thrift,вАЭ why central banking is fundamentally incompatible with free markets, and how fiat money fuels wealth inequality while convincing people capitalism is to blame. They also dive into socialism, Bitcoin as a savings technology, lowering time preference, and the future of Bitcoin scaling technologies like Lightning and ARK.

The conversation also covers Bitcoin Is Venice, the upcoming second edition of the book, and why Bitcoin adoption may happen not through ideology вАФ but through people slowly rediscovering common sense.

FOLLOW ALLEN: https://x.com/allenf32

PARTNERS & DISCOUNTS:

  • LEDN: Bitcoin-backed lending. Go to ledn.io/walker and unlock liquidity WITHOUT selling your bitcoin.

item.description:

item.title: The Bitcoin Infinity Show вАУ Defending Bitcoin: Cybersecurity for the Monetary Grid | Luke de Wolf | BIS #202
item.description:
Luke de Wolf joins the Bitcoin Infinity Show to talk about his upcoming book Defending Bitcoin: Industrial-Grade Cybersecurity for the Monetary Grid, the cybersecurity case for treating Bitcoin as critical infrastructure, and why arbitrary data on Bitcoin should be considered a textbook availability vulnerability. The conversation covers Luke's reversal on BIP-110, the governance concentration around Bitcoin Core, and why decentralized mining through Ocean, Datum, and Stratum V2 is essential for keeping the network resilient. Knut and Luke also preview BTC Prague, the Dublin conference, and the upcoming BTCHEL event in Helsinki this September, with its tracks on Nordic sovereignty, mining and energy, and Bitcoin for human rights.

Connect with Luke:

https://x.com/lukedewolf

https://primal.net/luke

https://btchel.com/

item.title: Bitcoin.com News Interviews вАУ Reynolds, SoFi SVP вАФ Why Most New Stablecoins Are Doomed to Fail
item.description:
Ben Reynolds was Silvergate's first Bitcoin-side hire back in 2016 and the company's president when it wound down in 2023. After stops at BVNK and BitGo, he joined SoFi six months ago to build out commercial banking at the intersection of TradFi and digital assets.

In this conversation, Reynolds explains why most new stablecoins are doomed without users or distribution, what makes SoFi's 15 million members and $50B balance sheet an unfair edge, and why the next wave of stablecoin innovation belongs to regulated banks.

We cover:

  • Why stablecoins without users or distribution are "almost doomed to fail"
  • The three legs of the stool: members, Galileo, and commercial banking
  • How the GENIUS Act pulls stablecoin innovation back into regulated banks
  • Why bank-to-bank stablecoin interoperability is the next big problem
  • What SoFi's $50B balance sheet means for counterparty risk
  • Why lending is the next frontier after payments

Filmed at Bitcoin 2026 in Las Vegas.

Host: David Sencil

рЯОІ Listen now and follow the show for more conversations with the builders shaping the future of Bitcoin, stablecoins, and digital finance.

]]>

item.title: Bitcoin Optech Podcast вАУ Bitcoin Optech: Newsletter #404 Recap
item.description:

News

вЧП Bitcoin Core #33796 (37:11)

вЧП Bitcoin Core #21283 (39:30)

вЧП BIPs #2150 (45:30)

вЧП Eclair #3144 (52:30)

item.title: Bitcoin.com News Weekly Update вАУ Coinbase Outage, Prediction Markets, BlackRock on Ethereum & Stablecoin Yield Wars
item.description: <![CDATA[This week, Graham Stone and Alex Richardson break down the biggest stories in crypto, from CoinbaseвАЩs AWS-related outage to signs of risk appetite returning across Bitcoin, altcoins and NFTs.They also dig into the rise of prediction markets, how TradFi giants are moving into event contracts, BlackRockвАЩs push to tokenize money market funds on Ethereum, and why stablecoin yield has become one of the key political fights in U.S. crypto regulation.Plus, Robert Kiyosaki is back with another doomer warning вАФ this time about baby boomers facing severe financial pressure.Topics covered:вАҐ Coinbase outage and crypto infrastructure riskвАҐ Bitcoin, altcoins and NFTs catching bidsвАҐ Prediction markets, manipulation and TradFi adoptionвАҐ BlackRock tokenized money market funds on EthereumвАҐ Stablecoin yield, banks and U.S. Treasury demandвАҐ Robert KiyosakiвАЩs warning for baby boomers00:30 - Market Updates: S&P 500, NASDAQ, Bitcoin01:29 - Altcoins and NFT Market Trends02:11 - Coinbase Outage Explanation03:03 - Technical Details of the Outage05:27 - Public Reaction and Speculation07:20 - Prediction Markets and Traditional Finance09:31 - ETFs and Crypto Legislation12:29 - BlackRock's Tokenized Funds15:13 - Clarity Act and Crypto Regulation19:25 - Robert Kiyosaki's Predictions]]>

item.title: VERIFIED by Bitcoin Suisse вАУ Can Bitcoin Escape the Quantum Black Hole?
item.description:
Is quantum computing Bitcoin's biggest hidden risk - and is anyone actually solving it? In this episode, Dominic Weibel and Luca Gnos sit down with Charles Edwards, founder of Capriole Investments, to break down how geopolitics, macro conditions, and quantum risk are shaping Bitcoin's next move. Edwards shares his onchain valuation tools pointing to a ~43% discount from fair value, explains why institutions now dominate Bitcoin's demand structure, and makes the case that quantum risk is already suppressing price - with a potential major repricing on the horizon within 12вАУ24 months.

]]>

item.title: Bitcoin Audible вАУ Chat_167 - Why Your Keys Matter with Seed
item.description:
"If you think of River or Gemini or Coinbase or whatever, think of all of those as silos and how people custody Bitcoin. If some sort of compromise were to come into the Bitcoin ecosystem at the private key generation level, that could create an incident that could set Bitcoin back by 10 or more years because it would be almost indistinguishable from a protocol compromise and that we would be talking about Bitcoin being hacked again and not individual custodians being hacked. So I think we need to decentralise how people do Bitcoin custody as much as possible and what better way by leveraging private freedom preserving open source software."

~ Seed

In todayвАЩs chat, I sat down with Seed, the creator of the SeedSigner project, and his backstory is completely wild.

He told the story of how he got pulled into Bitcoin through a forensic case tied to a local Silk Road user, why he panic sold in 2017, and how that path eventually led him back to the basics and into building one of the most respected DIY signers in the space. But the real tension in this episode is the part most people skip over: what actually keeps your coins safe when things get messy in the real world.

We talk about the grim reality of wrench attacks, what actually happens when law enforcement flips a house upside down with a search warrant, and how a former forensics cop secures his own wealth. If you think a standard hardware wallet hidden in a sock drawer is enough, hearing his threat models might completely rewrite your long-term storage plans. And yes, we got into a few stories that made me think about my own setup a lot differently.

Guest Links

  • Seed on X (Link: https://x.com/sesi_the_man)
  • SeedSigner on X (Link: https://x.com/SeedSigner)
  • Today in Bitcoin History on X (Link: https://x.com/daily_btc_lore)

item.title: Krypto Corner вАУ Bitcoin, ETF und NFT: Sicher investieren - Krypto verstehen und sicher starten вАУ Bonus zu Kapitel 32:Die Hardware-Wallet-Falle, Airdrops & Revoke.cash
item.description:
Wie schnell kannst du alles verlieren?¬†In dieser 20-min√Љtigen Bonus-Analyse verhindern unsere KI-Moderatoren in letzter Sekunde den totalen Ruin. Tom will aus purer Gier ("FOMO") einen vermeintlich kostenlosen Airdrop einsammeln und ignoriert dabei die Warnzeichen auf dem Display seiner Hardware-Wallet. Wir zeigen dir, warum dein Hardware-Ledger dich nicht besch√Љtzt, wenn du dem Teufel freiwillig die T√Љr √ґffnest.

Lerne das t√ґdlichste Konzept im Web3 kennen: Die "Unlimited Approvals". Erfahre, warum das blinde Best√§tigen von manipulierten Smart Contracts einer Blankovollmacht gleichkommt, die deine Wallet in Millisekunden restlos leersaugt.

Das absolute Highlight:¬†Der Steuer-Horror nach dem Diebstahl. Wir decken auf, warum das deutsche Finanzamt kein Mitleid mit gehackten Krypto-Nutzern hat und gestohlene Coins als reines "Privatvergn√Љgen" wertet. Erfahre, warum dich ein Hack f√Љr Krypto-Gewinne aus der Vergangenheit direkt in die Insolvenz treiben kann! Au√Яerdem verraten wir dir die eiserne Isolations-Taktik der Profis: Wie du "Burner Wallets" (Wegwerf-Wallets) und "Revoke.cash" nutzt, um dein digitales Haus sauber und sicher zu halten.

Die Highlights dieser Bonus-Analyse:

  • Unlimited Approvals:¬†Wie Krypto-Hacker deine Geldb√ґrse dauerhaft anzapfen.

  • Die Hardware-Wallet-Falle:¬†Warum der Tresor dumm ist, wenn du die T√Љr aufh√§ltst.

  • Privatvergn√Љgen:¬†Warum der Staat gehackte Krypto-Coins nicht als steuerlichen Verlust anerkennt.

  • Burner Wallets:¬†Die Isolations-Taktik der Profis.

  • Revoke.cash:¬†So putzt du dein digitales Haus.

Der Ton wechselt von extremer Panik und Gefahr zu einer eiskalten, technischen Lehrstunde √Љber digitale Hygiene und ruin√ґse Steuerfallen!

рЯЪА¬†BUCH-RELEASE:¬†Lerne die W√§chter-Tricks mit¬†вАЮKrypto verstehen вАУ sicher startenвАЬ (Band 1)¬†von Sascha Swen Heiter.

рЯФ•¬†EXKLUSIVE BUCH-PREVIEW:¬†Dieser Podcast bietet dir einen exklusiven Vorab-Einblick in das Standardwerk der Krypto-Welt.¬†Exklusiv:¬†Nur hier im Podcast erf√§hrst du Details und Insider-Wissen aus den 33 Kapiteln des Buchs.Deep Dive:¬†Wir gehen tiefer als jeder Blogpost. Die genauen Anleitungen zur Nutzung von Revoke-Tools und juristische Notfallpl√§ne f√Љr das Finanzamt findest du im offiziellen Buch. Es ist ab sofort √Љberall erh√§ltlich!

вЪЦпЄП¬†Rechtlicher Hinweis & Haftungsausschluss (ESSENZIELL):¬†S√§mtliche Inhalte dieses Podcasts dienen ausschlie√Яlich der allgemeinen Information und Unterhaltung. Alle Angaben erfolgen ohne Gew√§hr.

  • Keine Beratung:¬†Dieser Podcast stellt zu keinem Zeitpunkt eine Anlageberatung, Rechtsberatung oder Steuerberatung dar.

  • Eigenverantwortung:¬†Krypto-Investments sind hochriskant und k√ґnnen zum Totalverlust f√Љhren. Jeder H√ґrer handelt auf eigene Gefahr und Verantwortung.

  • Steuern:¬†Die steuerliche Behandlung von Kryptow√§hrungen ist hochkomplex. Bitte konsultiere zwingend einen qualifizierten Steuerberater f√Љr die aktuelle Rechtslage in der DACH-Region (Deutschland, √Цsterreich, Schweiz) f√Љr die Jahre 2025/2026.

  • Haftung:¬†Eine Haftung f√Љr Sch√§den, die aus der Nutzung der hier bereitgestellten Informationen entstehen, wird ausdr√Љcklich ausgeschlossen.

Basiert auf dem Buch von¬†Sascha Swen Heiter. Abonniere den Podcast f√Љr dein w√ґchentliches Krypto-Update!

item.title: All In Bitcoin вАУ 031 Jameson Lopp: Why We Choose Convenience Over Freedom (Nostr, Privacy & Bitcoin) I All in Bitcoin w/CK
item.description:
рЯОЩпЄП Jameson Lopp: Why We Choose Convenience Over Freedom (Nostr, Privacy & Bitcoin)

Freedom is being traded for convenience, and most people donвАЩt even notice.

In Part 2 with Jameson Lopp, we go deep into freedom tech, privacy tools, censorship-resistant communication, and the future of digital sovereignty.

Jameson explains why convenience is the easiest way to lose freedom, why tools like Signal, GrapheneOS, Linux, Bitcoin, and Nostr matter, and why freedom tech only wins if people actually use it.

We also discuss Telegram vs Signal, Nostr beyond вАЬjust another Twitter,вАЭ Bitcoin self-custody, ETFs, privacy, and the growing risks of centralization.

This is a conversation about liberty, communication, responsibility, and the tools we may need before itвАЩs too late.

item.title: BTC-ECHO Recap | Top-Stories zu Bitcoin & Kryptow√§hrungen вАУ Wir sind umgezogen!
No Second Best - A Bitcoin Podcast вАУ Did the Federal Reserve Just Open the Door to Bitcoin?
item.description:
Jamie Dimon just went on national television to push back against Bitcoin and crypto legislation. Hours later, a Bitcoin exchange gained direct access to the Federal Reserve's settlement rails.

At the same time, Morgan Stanley filed for a spot Bitcoin ETF while geopolitical tensions escalated in the Middle East and Bitcoin surged.

This episode breaks down the growing conflict between the traditional banking system and the emerging Bitcoin financial infrastructure, and why the global monetary system may already be bending toward neutral, hard money.

]]>

item.title: Bald in Bitcoin вАУ Bald in Bitcoin - Folge 12: Warum sparen Dich nicht mehr sch√Љtzt
item.description:
Thema der Folge

In dieser Folge geht es um die Frage, warum klassisches Sparen heute nicht mehr automatisch sch√Љtzt.

Fr√Љher konnten Sparer oft reale Kaufkraft aufbauen, weil die Zinsen oberhalb der Inflation lagen. Heute ist das h√§ufig anders. Viele Menschen sehen zwar weiterhin stabile Zahlen auf dem Konto, verlieren aber schleichend Kaufkraft.

Kerngedanken Sparen ist nicht falsch, aber es sch√Љtzt heute nicht mehr automatisch. Entscheidend ist nicht der nominale Kontostand, sondern die Kaufkraft. Zinsen m√Љssen immer im Verh√§ltnis zur Inflation betrachtet werden. Offizielle Inflation ist ein Durchschnitt, aber nicht unbedingt deine pers√ґnliche Realit√§t. Ein Bankkonto f√Љhlt sich sicher an, kann aber real Kaufkraft verlieren. Bitcoin ist f√Љr viele Menschen der Versuch, Wert langfristig zu speichern. Bitcoin kann als Werttransport √Љber Zeit und Raum verstanden werden. Zentrale S√§tze aus der Folge

вАЮDas System hat sich ver√§ndert, aber unser Denken nicht.вАЬ

вАЮZinsen sind das, was du bekommst. Inflation ist das, was du verlierst.вАЬ

вАЮInflation ist immer pers√ґnlich. Nicht das, was in der Statistik steht, sondern das, was bei dir im Alltag passiert.вАЬ

вАЮWir verwechseln Stabilit√§t mit Sicherheit.вАЬ

вАЮBitcoin ist kein schneller Gewinn. Es ist eine M√ґglichkeit, Wert zu bewahren вАУ √Љber Generationen hinweg.вАЬ

Vor-Ort Kurs Hinweis: Am 30.05.2026 findet mein Vor-Ort-Kurs statt: Bitcoin f√Љr Einsteiger вАУ digitales Geld f√Љr analog Geborene F√Љr alle, die Bitcoin ruhig, verst√§ndlich und ohne Fachchinesisch verstehen m√ґchten. Link: https://bitucation.com/kurs/vor-ort-bitcoin-fuer-einsteiger-digitales-geld-fuer-analog-geborene/

Ausblick

In der n√§chsten Folge geht es darum, warum der Staat dieses Geldsystem sogar braucht вАУ und warum sich daran so schnell nichts √§ndern wird.

]]>
item.itunes:summary:
Thema der Folge

In dieser Folge geht es um die Frage, warum klassisches Sparen heute nicht mehr automatisch sch√Љtzt.

Fr√Љher konnten Sparer oft reale Kaufkraft aufbauen, weil die Zinsen oberhalb der Inflation lagen. Heute ist das h√§ufig anders. Viele Menschen sehen zwar weiterhin stabile Zahlen auf dem Konto, verlieren aber schleichend Kaufkraft.

Kerngedanken Sparen ist nicht falsch, aber es sch√Љtzt heute nicht mehr automatisch. Entscheidend ist nicht der nominale Kontostand, sondern die Kaufkraft. Zinsen m√Љssen immer im Verh√§ltnis zur Inflation betrachtet werden. Offizielle Inflation ist ein Durchschnitt, aber nicht unbedingt deine pers√ґnliche Realit√§t. Ein Bankkonto f√Љhlt sich sicher an, kann aber real Kaufkraft verlieren. Bitcoin ist f√Љr viele Menschen der Versuch, Wert langfristig zu speichern. Bitcoin kann als Werttransport √Љber Zeit und Raum verstanden werden. Zentrale S√§tze aus der Folge

вАЮDas System hat sich ver√§ndert, aber unser Denken nicht.вАЬ

вАЮZinsen sind das, was du bekommst. Inflation ist das, was du verlierst.вАЬ

вАЮInflation ist immer pers√ґnlich. Nicht das, was in der Statistik steht, sondern das, was bei dir im Alltag passiert.вАЬ

вАЮWir verwechseln Stabilit√§t mit Sicherheit.вАЬ

вАЮBitcoin ist kein schneller Gewinn. Es ist eine M√ґglichkeit, Wert zu bewahren вАУ √Љber Generationen hinweg.вАЬ

Vor-Ort Kurs Hinweis: Am 30.05.2026 findet mein Vor-Ort-Kurs statt: Bitcoin f√Љr Einsteiger вАУ digitales Geld f√Љr analog Geborene F√Љr alle, die Bitcoin ruhig, verst√§ndlich und ohne Fachchinesisch verstehen m√ґchten. Link: https://bitucation.com/kurs/vor-ort-bitcoin-fuer-einsteiger-digitales-geld-fuer-analog-geborene/

Ausblick

In der n√§chsten Folge geht es darum, warum der Staat dieses Geldsystem sogar braucht вАУ und warum sich daran so schnell nichts √§ndern wird.

]]>

item.title: Thank God for Bitcoin вАУ To The Unknown Pod Episode 42: Bitcoin and A World At War
item.description:
In this episode, Jordan Bush and Ryan Finlay explore the complex intersections of global conflict, economic warfare, and Bitcoin's role as a resilient alternative in a world at war. They analyze recent Middle East tensions, the decline of the petrodollar, and how Bitcoin offers a form of financial sovereignty amidst geopolitical chaos.

]]>
item.itunes:summary:

They analyze recent Middle East tensions, the decline of the petrodollar, and how Bitcoin offers a form of financial sovereignty amidst geopolitical chaos.

]]>
